sn22.87:1.1: At one time the *Bhagavā* was dwelling at Rājagaha, in the Bamboo Grove, the Squirrels’ Feeding Place.
sn22.87:1.11: ‘It would be good, *bhante*, if the *Bhagavā* would come to the *bhikkhu* Vakkali out of compassion.’”
sn22.87:1.2: Now at that time the venerable Vakkali was dwelling in a potter’s workshop, sick, suffering, gravely ill.
sn22.87:1.3: Then the venerable Vakkali addressed his attendants:
sn22.87:1.4: “Come, friends, go to the *Bhagavā*. Having approached, bow with your heads at the feet of the *Bhagavā* in my name:
sn22.87:1.5: ‘Vakkali, *bhante*, the *bhikkhu*, is sick, suffering, gravely ill. He bows with his head at the feet of the *Bhagavā*.’
sn22.87:1.7: ‘It would be good, *bhante*, if the *Bhagavā* would come to the *bhikkhu* Vakkali out of compassion.’”
sn22.87:1.8: “Yes, friend,” those *bhikkhus* replied to the venerable Vakkali. Then they went to the *Bhagavā*; having approached and paid homage to the *Bhagavā*, they sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, those *bhikkhus* said this to the *Bhagavā*:
sn22.87:1.9: “Vakkali, *bhante*, the *bhikkhu*, is sick, suffering, gravely ill. He bows with his head at the feet of the *Bhagavā*;
sn22.87:10.2: “Come, *bhikkhus*, let us go to the Black Rock on the side of Isigili,
sn22.87:10.3: where the clansman Vakkali has used the knife.”
sn22.87:10.5: Then the *Bhagavā*, together with many *bhikkhus*, went to the Black Rock on the side of Isigili.
sn22.87:10.6: The *Bhagavā* saw the venerable Vakkali from afar, lying on the cot with his shoulder twisted aside.
sn22.87:11.1: Now at that time a smoky darkness, a murky darkness, kept going east, kept going west, kept going north, kept going south, kept going upward, kept going downward, kept going to the intermediate directions.
sn22.87:11.3: “Do you see, *bhikkhus*, that smoky darkness, that murky darkness, going east … going to the intermediate directions?”
sn22.87:11.5: “That, *bhikkhus*, is Māra the Evil One, searching for the consciousness [*viññāṇa*] of the clansman Vakkali:
sn22.87:11.6: ‘Where is the consciousness [*viññāṇa*] of the clansman Vakkali established?’
sn22.87:11.7: But with consciousness [*viññāṇa*] unestablished, *bhikkhus*, the clansman Vakkali has become fully quenched [*parinibbuto*].”
sn22.87:2.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having robed himself and taken bowl and robe, went to the venerable Vakkali.
sn22.87:2.10: “Indeed, *bhante*, I have quite a lot of worry, quite a lot of regret.”
sn22.87:2.11: “But Vakkali, does your own self not reproach you in regard to ethical conduct?”
sn22.87:2.12: “No indeed, *bhante*, my own self does not reproach me in regard to ethical conduct.”
sn22.87:2.13: “If, Vakkali, your own self does not reproach you in regard to ethical conduct, then what worry do you have, and what regret?”
sn22.87:2.14: “For a long time, *bhante*, I have wanted to approach the *Bhagavā* to see him, but there has not been in my body enough strength for me to approach the *Bhagavā* to see him.”
sn22.87:2.2: The venerable Vakkali saw the *Bhagavā* coming from afar. Seeing him, he shifted on the cot.
sn22.87:2.3: Then the *Bhagavā* said this to the venerable Vakkali:
sn22.87:2.4: “Enough, Vakkali, do not shift about on the cot.
sn22.87:2.5: There are seats prepared here; I will sit there.”
sn22.87:2.6: The *Bhagavā* sat down on the prepared seat. Seated, the *Bhagavā* said this to the venerable Vakkali:
sn22.87:2.7: “I hope, Vakkali, that it is bearable for you, I hope you are managing; I hope the painful *vedanās* are subsiding, not worsening; that their subsiding is evident, not their worsening?”
sn22.87:2.8: “It is not bearable for me, *bhante*, I am not managing. Strong painful *vedanās* are worsening, not subsiding; their worsening is evident, not their subsiding.”
sn22.87:2.9: “I hope, Vakkali, that you have no worry, no regret?”
sn22.87:3.1: “Enough, Vakkali. What is there for you in seeing this foul body?
sn22.87:3.4: For seeing the *dhamma*, Vakkali, one sees me;
sn22.87:3.5: and seeing me, one sees the *dhamma*.
sn22.87:4.18: he understands: ‘there is no more of this state of being.’”
sn22.87:4.4: “But what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], is that *dukkha* or *sukha*?”
sn22.87:4.6: “But what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, and subject to change, is it fit to regard it thus:
sn22.87:4.7: ‘This is mine, this I am, this is my self’?”
sn22.87:5.1: Then the *Bhagavā*, having instructed the venerable Vakkali with this instruction, rose from his seat and departed for Mount Gijjhakūṭa.
sn22.87:5.11: “Surely, *bhante*, being well liberated, he will be liberated.”
sn22.87:5.13: Having said this, they paid homage to the *Bhagavā*, circled him keeping their right side toward him, and vanished right there.
sn22.87:5.2: Then, not long after the *Bhagavā* had left, the venerable Vakkali addressed his attendants:
sn22.87:5.3: “Come, friends, lift me onto the cot and go to the Black Rock on the side of Isigili.
sn22.87:5.4: For how could someone like me think it proper to die inside a dwelling?”
sn22.87:5.5: “Yes, friend,” those *bhikkhus* replied to the venerable Vakkali. Then, having lifted the venerable Vakkali onto the cot, they went to the Black Rock on the side of Isigili.
sn22.87:5.6: And the *Bhagavā* stayed that night and the rest of that day on Mount Gijjhakūṭa.
sn22.87:5.7: Then, when the night had advanced, two *devatās*, of stunning beauty, illuminating the whole of Gijjhakūṭa, approached the *Bhagavā* … and stood to one side.
sn22.87:5.8: Standing to one side, one *devatā* said this to the *Bhagavā*:
sn22.87:6.1: Then, when that night had passed, the *Bhagavā* addressed the *bhikkhus*:
sn22.87:6.2: “Come, *bhikkhus*, go to the *bhikkhu* Vakkali, and having approached, say this to the *bhikkhu* Vakkali:
sn22.87:7.1: ‘Hear, friend Vakkali, the word of the *Bhagavā* and of the two *devatās*.
sn22.87:7.10: Your death will not be bad; your passing away will not be bad.’”
sn22.87:7.11: “Yes, *bhante*,” those *bhikkhus* replied to the *Bhagavā*. Then they went to the venerable Vakkali, and having approached, they said this to him:
sn22.87:7.12: “Hear, friend Vakkali, the word of the *Bhagavā* and of the two *devatās*.”
sn22.87:7.2: Tonight, friend, when the night had advanced, two *devatās*, of stunning beauty, illuminating the whole of Gijjhakūṭa, approached the *Bhagavā*; having approached and paid homage to the *Bhagavā*, they stood to one side.
sn22.87:7.3: Standing to one side, friend, one *devatā* said this to the *Bhagavā*—
sn22.87:7.4: Vakkali, *bhante*, the *bhikkhu*, is intent on liberation.
sn22.87:7.6: Surely, *bhante*, being well liberated, he will be liberated.
sn22.87:7.7: And the *Bhagavā* said this to you, friend Vakkali—
sn22.87:8.1: Then the venerable Vakkali addressed his attendants:
sn22.87:8.10: And the *Bhagavā* said this to you, friend Vakkali:
sn22.87:8.13: Your death will not be bad; your passing away will not be bad.’”
sn22.87:8.14: “Then, friends, in my name bow with your heads at the feet of the *Bhagavā*:
sn22.87:8.15: ‘Vakkali, *bhante*, the *bhikkhu*, is sick, suffering, gravely ill.
sn22.87:8.16: He bows with his head at the feet of the *Bhagavā*.’
sn22.87:8.19: About that, *bhante*, I have no doubt.
sn22.87:8.2: “Come, friends, lower me from the cot.
sn22.87:8.20: I have no uncertainty that what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:8.21: I have no uncertainty that, in what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, and subject to change, there is for me no desire, no passion, no fondness.’
sn22.87:8.23: About them, *bhante*, I have no doubt.
sn22.87:8.24: I have no uncertainty that what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:8.25: I have no uncertainty that, in what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, and subject to change, there is for me no desire, no passion, no fondness.’
sn22.87:8.28: About them, *bhante*, I have no doubt.
sn22.87:8.29: I have no uncertainty that what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:8.3: For how could someone like me think it proper to listen to the message of that *Bhagavā* while sitting on a high seat?”
sn22.87:8.30: I have no uncertainty that, in what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, and subject to change, there is for me no desire, no passion, no fondness.’
sn22.87:8.32: About that, *bhante*, I have no doubt.
sn22.87:8.33: I have no uncertainty that what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:8.34: I have no uncertainty that, in what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, and subject to change, there is for me no desire, no passion, no fondness.’”
sn22.87:8.35: “Yes, friend,” those *bhikkhus* replied to the venerable Vakkali, and departed.
sn22.87:8.36: Then, not long after those *bhikkhus* had departed, the venerable Vakkali used the knife.
sn22.87:8.4: “Yes, friend,” those *bhikkhus* replied to the venerable Vakkali. Then they lowered the venerable Vakkali from the cot.
sn22.87:8.5: “Tonight, friend, when the night had advanced, two *devatās* … stood to one side.
sn22.87:8.6: Standing to one side, friend, one *devatā* said this to the *Bhagavā*:
sn22.87:8.9: ‘Surely, *bhante*, being well liberated, he will be liberated.’
sn22.87:9.1: Then those *bhikkhus* went to the *Bhagavā*; having approached, they sat down to one side. Sitting to one side, those *bhikkhus* said this to the *Bhagavā*:
sn22.87:9.13: About that, *bhante*, I have no doubt.
sn22.87:9.14: I have no uncertainty that what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:9.15: I have no uncertainty that, in what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, and subject to change, there is for me no desire, no passion, no fondness.’”
sn22.87:9.2: “Vakkali, *bhante*, the *bhikkhu*, is sick, suffering, gravely ill.
sn22.87:9.3: He bows with his head at the feet of the *Bhagavā*;
sn22.87:9.6: About that, *bhante*, I have no doubt.
sn22.87:9.7: I have no uncertainty that what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*] is *dukkha*.
sn22.87:9.8: I have no uncertainty that, in what is impermanent [*aniccaṁ*], *dukkha*, and subject to change, there is for me no desire, no passion, no fondness.
